# Beudox HR Management System - Technical Context

## Architecture Overview

This is a modern React-based frontend application using:
- **SPA Architecture**: Single-page application with client-side routing
- **Component-Based UI**: Modular component structure with reusable UI elements
- **Server State Management**: TanStack Query for API data fetching and caching
- **Styling System**: Utility-first CSS with Tailwind and design system components

## Key Architectural Decisions

### State Management
- Uses TanStack Query for server state (API data)
- Local component state with React hooks
- No global state management library - kept simple for this scale

### Component Organization
- Feature-based folder structure under `src/components/`
- Shared UI components in `src/components/ui/` (Shadcn/ui)
- Layout components for consistent app structure

### Routing
- React Router DOM for client-side routing
- Page-based components in `src/pages/`
- Nested routes for complex features like evaluations

### Data Flow
- Direct API calls to Supabase using generated TypeScript types
- Real-time subscriptions for notifications and updates
- Optimistic updates for better UX

## Core Business Logic

### Authentication & Authorization
- Supabase Auth for user management
- Role-based access control (CEO, HR Manager, Team Lead, Employee)
- Permission checks at component level

### Employee Management
- CRUD operations for employee profiles
- Invitation system with email notifications
- Role assignment and department management

### Leave Management
- Leave type configuration (annual, sick, etc.)
- Balance tracking and automatic calculations
- Approval workflow with notifications

### Performance Evaluations
- **Quarterly Evaluations**: Formal reviews with recommendations
- **Daily Evaluations**: Peer feedback system
- Timeline view combining both evaluation types
- Role-based visibility (managers see more details)

### Settings Management
- Company-wide configuration
- Department and role setup
- Notification preferences
- Expense category management

## Key Files and Their Purposes

### Entry Points
- `src/main.tsx`: Application bootstrap, routing setup
- `src/pages/Index.tsx`: Main dashboard/home page

### Core Components
- `src/components/layout/AppLayout.tsx`: Main app shell with sidebar and topbar
- `src/components/layout/AppSidebar.tsx`: Navigation sidebar
- `src/components/BeudoxLogo.tsx`: Logo component with variants

### Feature Components
- `src/components/leave/ApplyLeaveModal.tsx`: Leave request form
- `src/components/evaluations/EvaluationTimeline.tsx`: Evaluation history display
- `src/components/hr-policies/RichTextEditor.tsx`: Policy editing interface
- `src/components/SearchableEmployeeSelect.tsx`: Employee selection dropdown

### Utilities
- `src/lib/utils.ts`: Common utility functions (cn for class merging)
- `src/lib/role-access.ts`: Permission checking logic
- `src/lib/leave-utils.ts`: Leave balance calculations
- `src/lib/notifications.ts`: Notification sending logic

### Integrations
- `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts`: Supabase client configuration
- `src/integrations/supabase/types.ts`: Generated TypeScript types

## Database Schema Highlights

### Core Tables
- `employees`: User profiles with roles and departments
- `leave_requests`: Leave applications with approval status
- `evaluations`: Quarterly performance reviews
- `daily_evaluations`: Peer feedback records
- `companies`: Multi-tenant company data
- `departments`: Organizational structure
- `roles`: Permission definitions

### Key Relationships
- Employees belong to companies and departments
- Evaluations link employees to evaluators
- Leave requests have approval chains
- Daily evaluations support peer-to-peer feedback

## Configuration Files

- `vite.config.ts`: Build configuration
- `tailwind.config.ts`: Styling system setup
- `tsconfig.json`: TypeScript compiler options
- `supabase/config.toml`: Database configuration

## Development Workflow

- Component development follows Shadcn/ui patterns
- TypeScript strict mode enabled
- ESLint for code quality
- Vitest for unit testing
- Supabase migrations for database changes

## Security Considerations

- Row Level Security (RLS) policies in Supabase
- Client-side permission checks
- Secure environment variable handling
- Input validation and sanitization

## Performance Optimizations

- Query caching with TanStack Query
- Lazy loading of routes and components
- Optimized bundle splitting
- Image optimization and CDN usage
